title: My Sister My Sitter

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with Homer's characteristic mishap
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation followed by screaming
jokes.explanation: Iconic character moment that perfectly captures Homer's essence in one word. High character integration and economy.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: opening day at the world's first two-story outhouse. [Man] Oh, God! Stop!
jokes.setup: Newscast promotes opening of world's first two-story outhouse
jokes.punchline: Immediate horrified reaction from someone using it
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd concept with perfect timing on the horrified reaction. Great visual comedy implied.

dialogue.speaker: TV Host
dialogue.line: We now go live to the opening of the world's first two-story outhouse.

dialogue.speaker: Man
dialogue.line: Ahhhhhhh!

jokes.text: Okay. How many of you are here for shoulder surgery, huh? - [All Groaning] - Got ya!
jokes.setup: Nurse asking patients about shoulder surgery in medical setting
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's a prank on patients expecting medical care
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about medical care anxiety, but relatively predictable prank setup.

dialogue.speaker: Comedy Nurse
dialogue.line: So you're all here for shoulder surgery?

dialogue.speaker: Patients
dialogue.line: Yes, doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Comedy Nurse
dialogue.line: Well, I have some bad news. This is actually a prank show!

dialogue.speaker: Patients
dialogue.line: What?!

jokes.text: You didn't remember that. You just saw it on TV. The important thing is I didn't imagine it.
jokes.setup: Homer claims he remembered about the waterfront party
jokes.punchline: Homer's illogical defense when caught lying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - caught in an obvious lie but finds a technicality to save face. Great character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I remembered about the waterfront party!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, you didn't remember. You forgot completely.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's not true, Marge. I remembered it so hard, I forgot it.

jokes.text: That's a trust, Janie, a sacred trust.
jokes.setup: Lisa taking babysitting very seriously
jokes.punchline: Treating refrigerator privileges as sacred responsibility
jokes.explanation: Lisa's earnestness about trivial matters perfectly captures her character. The overstatement is endearing.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: As a babysitter, I take my responsibilities very seriously. The refrigerator is off-limits unless there's a genuine nutritional emergency.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: But Lisa, I'm starving!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Starving is not a genuine nutritional emergency. Starvation is a process.

jokes.text: [Snorts] All the best bands are affiliated with Satan.
jokes.setup: Reverend announces Christian rock band Testament
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual observation about Satan and good music
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart irreverence with excellent economy. Subversive comment delivered matter-of-factly in church setting.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I didn't know Satan was into good music.

jokes.text: [Man Coughing] [Clears Throat] The topic was love.
jokes.setup: Reverend offers discount for mentioning sermon topic
jokes.punchline: Awkward pause before someone reluctantly reveals the obvious topic
jokes.explanation: Simple setup/payoff about obvious sermon topics. Functional but unremarkable.

dialogue.speaker: Reverend Lovejoy
dialogue.line: Now, if you mention the sermon topic, you'll get a discount!

dialogue.speaker: Man
dialogue.line: Was it... God?

jokes.text: People often mistake me for nine.
jokes.setup: Lisa arguing she's mature for her age
jokes.punchline: Her example of maturity is being mistaken for one year older
jokes.explanation: Perfect child logic - Lisa's innocent misunderstanding of what constitutes maturity. Great character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I'm very mature for my age.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Prove it.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Well, last week someone thought I was nine instead of eight!

jokes.text: Homer, I've got a Fozzie of a bear of a problem.
jokes.setup: Ned starting to explain his problem
jokes.punchline: Ned's characteristically wholesome way of saying 'a hell of a problem'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ned-ism that perfectly captures his character - finding innocent ways to express strong emotions. High craft for character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Ned
dialogue.line: Well, I've got a heck of a problem.

jokes.text: Militants, huh? Well, if I were you, I'd kick their asses.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining his family's hostage situation
jokes.punchline: Homer's simplistic violent solution to international crisis
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer oversimplification of complex geopolitical situation. Good character moment but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Have you tried shooting it?

jokes.text: Oh, gee, I'd really love to want to help you, Flanders... but, uh, Marge was taken prisoner... in the... Holy Land, and-
jokes.setup: Homer trying to avoid helping Ned
jokes.punchline: Homer's terrible improvised excuse using Ned's own situation
jokes.explanation: Homer's lazy attempt to copy Ned's excuse shows his selfishness and stupidity. The hesitation makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Sorry Ned, I can't help you. I'd love to, but I'm... I'm dying.

dialogue.speaker: Ned
dialogue.line: Oh no! When?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Soon. Very soon. The doctors say I have... three weeks.

dialogue.speaker: Ned
dialogue.line: Homer, that's terrible! What can I do?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, you could help me with one last thing before I go...

dialogue.speaker: Ned
dialogue.line: Of course! Anything!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: ...but you just said you're dying too, so never mind.

jokes.text: Uh, I'll have to ask her.
jokes.setup: Ned asks if Lisa can babysit his kids
jokes.punchline: Homer pretends he needs to ask Lisa when she's standing right there begging to do it
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness to the obvious situation. Simple but effective misdirection.

dialogue.speaker: Ned
dialogue.line: Can Lisa babysit my kids?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'll have to ask her... Lisa!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, I'm right here. I'd love to babysit.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: She says yes.

jokes.text: Daddy says dice are wicked. We just move one space at a time. It's less fun that way.
jokes.setup: Flanders kids explaining their board game rules
jokes.punchline: Todd's matter-of-fact acceptance that their version is deliberately less fun
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of overly sheltered children accepting joyless rules. Todd's innocent acceptance makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Rod
dialogue.line: If you land on a blessed square, you move forward two spaces.

dialogue.speaker: Todd
dialogue.line: And if you land on a wicked square, you move backward three spaces and say a prayer of contrition.

dialogue.speaker: Rod
dialogue.line: The winner is whoever reaches the end first, unless they've been sinful, in which case they lose automatically.

dialogue.speaker: Todd
dialogue.line: We made it less fun on purpose.

jokes.text: [Both] Moth! Moth! Moth! Moth! Moth! Moth!
jokes.setup: Flanders boys encounter a moth
jokes.punchline: Completely disproportionate terror at harmless insect
jokes.explanation: Shows how sheltered the Flanders children are - terrified of completely harmless things.

dialogue.speaker: Rod
dialogue.line: A moth! A moth!

dialogue.speaker: Todd
dialogue.line: It's huge! It's gonna kill us!

dialogue.speaker: Rod
dialogue.line: We're doomed!

jokes.text: Don't worry. A moth is no more harmful than a ladybug. A ladybug? [Screaming]
jokes.setup: Lisa tries to calm their fears by comparing moth to ladybug
jokes.punchline: Boys are equally terrified of the supposedly harmless comparison
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure - Lisa's attempt to help makes it worse. Good timing on the immediate screaming.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: It's just like a ladybug. Ladybugs are harmless.

dialogue.speaker: Rod
dialogue.line: Ahhhhh!

dialogue.speaker: Todd
dialogue.line: Ahhhhh!

jokes.text: [Groans] They're gonna get eaten alive in middle school.
jokes.setup: Witnessing the Flanders boys' extreme sheltering
jokes.punchline: Lisa's dark but accurate prediction about their future
jokes.explanation: Lisa's mature observation about the boys' lack of preparation for real world. Dark but realistic.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Those boys are so sheltered, they'll probably end up as either delusional optimists or deeply disturbed individuals.

jokes.text: So long, Lisa! If anything goes wrong, just dial 911 - unless it's an emergency.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum giving babysitting advice
jokes.punchline: Contradictory instruction about when to call emergency services
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Chief Wiggum incompetence - perfect illogical instruction that sounds official but makes no sense. Excellent craft.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Now, if there's any trouble, just give me a call at the station.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: But don't call unless it's a real emergency.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: And don't call at all if I'm not there.

jokes.text: [Lisa] Okay, Chief! Enjoy Bob Saget! [Chuckles] It's Bob Seger. Aw, crap!
jokes.setup: Lisa mishears or misremembers the performer name
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's disappointed correction reveals he's excited about Bob Seger
jokes.explanation: Simple name confusion joke. Wiggum's disappointment adds character but it's fairly basic humor.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I'm so excited to see Bob Seger tonight!

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Bob Seger? Oh, you mean the Silver Bullet Band? That's gonna be great!

jokes.text: It's like a rent-a-car, Son. You get all the mileage you can... then ball it up and cram it through the mail slot.
jokes.setup: Bart asks why Homer is wearing tuxedo early
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely wrong understanding of rental car analogy applied to tuxedo
jokes.explanation: Homer's twisted logic creates absurd but internally consistent reasoning. Great character-specific stupidity.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad, why are you wearing a tuxedo at 6 in the morning?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I rented it.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: You rented it? When do you have to return it?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Return it? Bart, when you rent a tuxedo, you keep it.

jokes.text: I only wish my Volvo mechanic were as reliable. [Laughing] Good night, Lisa.
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert praising Lisa's reliability
jokes.punchline: His characteristic awkward laugh after inappropriate comparison
jokes.explanation: Typical Dr. Hibbert moment with his inappropriate comparisons followed by uncomfortable laughter.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Well, Lisa, you're as reliable as a condom machine in a girls' school. Hoo-hoo-hoo!

jokes.text: I sell peace of mind for a dollar an hour- two dollars after 9:00.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining babysitting is more than watching TV
jokes.punchline: Lisa's businesslike description of emotional service with specific pricing
jokes.explanation: Lisa's precocious understanding of service economy. The specific pricing structure makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Babysitting is more than just watching TV and eating chips. It's a service industry. I provide emotional support, conflict resolution, and creative play stimulation.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: My rates are: $5 an hour for basic childcare, $7.50 for educational activities, $10 for psychological counseling, and $15 if they need me to be a role model.

jokes.text: You could be Abe Lincoln's father's boss.
jokes.setup: Lisa complimenting Homer's formal appearance
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and convoluted compliment about historical hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully bizarre compliment structure. The specificity and historical reference make it uniquely funny.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, you look surprisingly distinguished in that tuxedo. You know, it's almost as if the feudal system's rigid class structure somehow found its way into modern formal wear, and you've managed to embody the aspirational qualities of a minor nobleman who's just been invited to court.

jokes.text: This is the greatest injustice in the history of the world!
jokes.setup: Bart learning Lisa will babysit him
jokes.punchline: Bart's dramatic overreaction comparing babysitting to historical injustices
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart melodrama - treating minor inconvenience as world-historical tragedy. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Lisa's gonna babysit me? That's like... that's like putting me under house arrest! It's a violation of my constitutional rights! This is worse than the Boston Tea Party, worse than the Trail of Tears, worse than that time I had to sit through a whole PTA meeting!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, it's just for two hours.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Two hours?! Two hours?! That's like a lifetime! A lifetime of tyranny! I'm calling the United Nations!

jokes.text: You're dead. You should wash up for dinner. To make it fun, you can use the Mr. Bubble. It'll be like giving your fingers a bubble bath. You are so dead.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to make babysitting fun for hostile Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's threats juxtaposed with Lisa's cheerful suggestions
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Lisa's earnest attempts and Bart's menacing responses. Creates uncomfortable tension.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I'm gonna tie you to a chair and pour syrup all over you!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Or we could play board games!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I'm gonna lock you in the basement!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: We could read poetry together!

jokes.text: I don't think we should be driving here. The mayor's yelling at us. Stop, you idiots! All right. All right. Geez!
jokes.setup: Homer driving through pedestrian mall
jokes.punchline: Mayor's frustrated reaction to obvious traffic violation
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness to driving rules. Functional physical comedy but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Mayor Quimby
dialogue.line: Homer! You can't drive through the pedestrian mall!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But Mr. Mayor, I'm late for work!

dialogue.speaker: Mayor Quimby
dialogue.line: That's no excuse! This is a safety hazard!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Oh, I love this pedestrian mall. There's practically no traffic. - [Horn Honking] - [Gasping]
jokes.setup: Marge comments positively about the lack of traffic
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicted by honking and them clearly being in traffic
jokes.explanation: Classic immediate contradiction joke - setup and punchline happen simultaneously

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: My, isn't it nice? No traffic at all.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah...

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: We're making excellent time!
[HONK HONK HONK - cars honking all around them, traffic jam visible]

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: I didn't say I was gonna eat them. I just wanted to look at them because they're so gross.
jokes.setup: Bart asking for more lima beans
jokes.punchline: Reveals he wants them not to eat but to disgust himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - finding perverse enjoyment in disgusting things. Good misdirection on his intentions.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Mom, can I have some more lima beans?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Of course, honey.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Actually, I don't want to eat them. I just want to look at them so I can disgust myself.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm a baby, huh? Well, then I'll act like a baby. Ga-ga! Goo-goo!
jokes.setup: Lisa calls Bart a baby
jokes.punchline: Bart's literal interpretation and exaggerated baby acting
jokes.explanation: Standard sibling antagonism with Bart's typical literal defiance. Functional but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, you're such a baby.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well if I'm a baby, then I'm gonna act like one!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Waaah! Waaah! Goo goo ga ga!

jokes.text: Even babies know how to open and close their mouths. You need a bib. [Gags] Oh, baby hate bib.
jokes.setup: Bart acting like a baby while eating
jokes.punchline: Lisa's practical response and Bart's continued baby talk while choking
jokes.explanation: Continuation of baby acting bit. Lisa's practical response is good but overall sequence is standard.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, you're going to choke doing that.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Goo goo ga ga!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I'm serious. You need to swallow before you breathe.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Waaah! Goo goo!

jokes.text: Well, I guess that'll make things more interesting for you now, won't it?
jokes.setup: Bart gives Maggie coffee ice cream with caffeine
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual acknowledgment that he's deliberately creating chaos
jokes.explanation: Bart's calculated mischief with understanding of consequences. His casual delivery makes the sabotage funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I gave Maggie coffee ice cream with caffeine in it.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ay caramba, what have I done?

jokes.text: Nah. I'm really more of a slacker.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting they're yuppies now
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest self-assessment rejecting the upward mobility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer self-awareness about his lack of ambition. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, we're yuppies now.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We are not! I work hard for a living. I have a job at the nuclear plant, I come home, I crack open a cold one, and I watch TV. If that makes me a yuppie, then so be it. But I know in my heart that I'm just an ordinary Joe who... who likes to... to enjoy himself.

jokes.text: The entire menu was personally approved by my secretary. [Laughing]
jokes.setup: Celebrity restaurant owner taking credit for menu
jokes.punchline: Admits he didn't actually do the work himself
jokes.explanation: Decent satire of celebrity endorsements where celebrities don't actually participate. Fairly standard comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Celebrity Chef
dialogue.line: Of course, I personally created every dish on this menu. Each recipe is a masterpiece born from my own culinary genius.

dialogue.speaker: Celebrity Chef
dialogue.line: Well, okay, fine. My sous chef actually made most of them. And my pastry chef did the desserts. And honestly, the line cooks probably did like 90% of the actual cooking.

jokes.text: Isn't that clever? It looks like a Cadillac drove right into the building. Help me.
jokes.setup: Marge admiring restaurant's car-crash themed decor
jokes.punchline: Reveals someone is actually trapped in what appears to be decoration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy subverting expectation. What looks like themed decor is actually genuine emergency. Excellent structure.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh, what a clever restaurant! Look at this authentic car crash décor!

dialogue.speaker: Trapped Person
dialogue.line: Help! Somebody help me! I've been trapped in here for three days!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: So realistic!

jokes.text: Hey, this isn't faux dive! [Scoffs] This is a dive.
jokes.setup: Moe commenting on his bar's new location
jokes.punchline: Moe's pride in his bar being authentically terrible rather than fashionably so
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - taking pride in genuine seediness over trendy imitation. Great character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: You know, I'm real proud of this place. It's authentically terrible, not like those trendy dive bars in the city. This dump didn't become a dump on purpose – it earned it through years of neglect and poor life choices.

jokes.text: You're a long way from home, yuppie boy. I'll start a tab.
jokes.setup: Moe confronting Homer about being out of place
jokes.punchline: Immediately switches to servile bartender mode for business
jokes.explanation: Good Moe characterization - tough guy posturing immediately abandoned for profit motive.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Homer, you don't belong here. This is a classy establishment.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: What? But I come here every day!

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Yeah, well— *notices customer entering* Welcome! Come right in, sir! Best drinks in town!

jokes.text: That's right. I want the 25-foot Italian party sub. And don't skimp on the vinegar.
jokes.setup: Bart ordering food to cause trouble for Lisa
jokes.punchline: Absurdly large sandwich order with specific condiment request
jokes.explanation: Good escalation in Bart's sabotage plan. The specific size and condiment detail add absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'd like to order a sandwich. Make it huge. Like, the size of a small car.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: And I want exactly 47 pickles on it. Not 46, not 48. Forty. Seven.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Oh, and put the mayo on the left side only. My sister's gonna hate this.

jokes.text: I'm using nonviolent resistance. Ugh! The idea that you would compare yourself to Mahatma Gandhi! Who?
jokes.setup: Bart claiming to use nonviolent resistance
jokes.punchline: Doesn't know who Gandhi is despite invoking the concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart - using sophisticated concepts without understanding them. Lisa's outrage makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I'm practicing nonviolent resistance, man. Just like Gandhi.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, who's Gandhi?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I dunno. But he sounds cool.

jokes.text: It's gonna be $225, plus tip. What? I didn't order any giant sub.
jokes.setup: Delivery of giant sub Lisa didn't order
jokes.punchline: Exorbitant price for Bart's prank order
jokes.explanation: Payoff to Bart's prank setup. The expensive price adds stakes but it's predictable escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Delivery Guy
dialogue.line: That'll be $8,347.50.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: But I didn't order this!

dialogue.speaker: Delivery Guy
dialogue.line: Says here it's for Bart Simpson. One 300-foot submarine sandwich.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart! This is your prank, you pay for it!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Eat my shorts, Lisa. I'm outta here!

jokes.text: Hey, hey! Are you ready to get rowdy? Somebody hired me for an emergency bachelor party.
jokes.setup: Another unexpected visitor at the door
jokes.punchline: Male stripper arriving for fake bachelor party booking
jokes.explanation: Escalation of Bart's prank campaign. The inappropriate service creates good cringe comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Who is it?

dialogue.speaker: Male Stripper
dialogue.line: Hey, I'm here for the bachelor party!

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: We didn't order a stripper.

dialogue.speaker: Male Stripper
dialogue.line: Yeah you did. Says right here—Simpson residence.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Well, there's no bachelor party here, pal.

dialogue.speaker: Male Stripper
dialogue.line: Aww, man. That's the third wrong address this week.

jokes.text: We got a report that a Lisa Simpson spotted a U.F.O. I didn't see any U.F. O! That's right, miss. You didn't.
jokes.setup: Government agents arriving about UFO report
jokes.punchline: Agent's ominous confirmation that she 'didn't' see anything
jokes.explanation: Good conspiracy comedy with menacing undertone. The agent's confirmation of denial is appropriately sinister.

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.line: We're here about your UFO report.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Yes, I saw a spacecraft land in our backyard.

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.line: No, you didn't.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: But I—

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.line: You didn't see anything.

jokes.text: I'm here to pick up the ambassador from Ghana. Well, he's not here! Nobody's here!
jokes.setup: Another confused service provider arrives
jokes.punchline: Diplomatic transportation for non-existent ambassador
jokes.explanation: Continues escalation of fake service calls. Diplomatic angle adds absurdity but sequence is getting repetitive.

dialogue.speaker: Driver
dialogue.line: I'm here to pick up Ambassador Krusty.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: There is no Ambassador Krusty. Krusty the Clown isn't a real ambassador.

dialogue.speaker: Driver
dialogue.line: Well, someone paid for diplomatic transportation. I've got the paperwork right here.

jokes.text: I said, 'Go to bed.' Yeah. 'Go to bread.' B-E-D! Bed! Oh! 'Bed'! Oh.
jokes.setup: Lisa ordering Bart to bed
jokes.punchline: Bart's deliberate misunderstanding of simple instruction
jokes.explanation: Classic annoying sibling tactic. Bart's fake confusion is predictable but true to character.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, it's time for bed.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Bed? I'm not tired!

jokes.text: You didn't say which bed!
jokes.setup: Bart getting into parents' bed instead of his own
jokes.punchline: Bart's literal interpretation excuse for continued defiance
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Bart's literal interpretation strategy. Shows his cleverness in finding loopholes.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Bart, what are you doing in our bed?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Well, you said I should get into bed with my parents more often.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We said no such thing!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: You said, 'Bart, you need to be more of a family man.' So here I am.

jokes.text: Okay, we'll call it even if I can just have some of that big sandwich.
jokes.setup: Stripper seeing aftermath of Lisa and Bart's fight
jokes.punchline: Pragmatic request for food instead of payment after witnessing chaos
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the giant sub. Stripper's practical response to the chaos is amusing.

dialogue.speaker: Male Stripper
dialogue.line: Forget the money. Just get me a sandwich.

jokes.text: [Gasps] Eww! Your arm! It's got extra corners! [Gasps] Oh, cool!
jokes.setup: Lisa seeing Bart's dislocated arm
jokes.punchline: Bart's excitement about his injury rather than pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect kid reaction to injury - fascination over concern. Lisa's description of 'extra corners' is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, your arm is dislocated!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I know! Isn't it cool? I can move it all around!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: You should be in excruciating pain.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Nah, I'm just excited I finally have a legitimate excuse to get out of gym class.

jokes.text: And you'll never babysit again!
jokes.setup: Bart realizing his injury gives him leverage
jokes.punchline: Bart's gleeful recognition that this will ruin Lisa's reputation
jokes.explanation: Bart's vindictive satisfaction in Lisa's downfall. Shows his understanding of consequences for her.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... I'm injured. That means I can get away with anything!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: And if Lisa tries to stop me, everyone will think SHE'S the bad guy for picking on a cripple!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ay caramba, this is gonna destroy her goody-two-shoes image!

jokes.text: Don't laugh at me. I was once like you.
jokes.setup: Homer falls in fountain, crowd laughs
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic declaration to laughing crowd
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment suggesting he was once competent. The dramatic delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why, you all think this is funny? Well, the joke's on you! I'm gonna go home and eat some donuts!

jokes.text: I'm banging my head. I'm gonna make the lump even bigger. [Sinister Laughing]
jokes.setup: Bart locked in bathroom, making noise
jokes.punchline: Bart deliberately injuring himself more to frame Lisa
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark escalation - Bart willing to hurt himself to hurt Lisa worse. The sinister laugh sells it.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Lisa! Lisa, help! I'm stuck!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, what happened?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I can't get out! My arm's stuck in the sink!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait... wait... if I break my other arm, everyone will think YOU did it!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ow! Ow! Ow! Lisa hurt me! Lisa hurt me!

jokes.text: Simpson? Look, we've already been out there tonight for a 'sisterectomy'... a case of severe butt rot and a leprechaun bite.
jokes.setup: Lisa calling 911 for help
jokes.punchline: Operator lists absurd previous calls from same address
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier prank with escalating absurdity. 'Butt rot' and 'leprechaun bite' are wonderfully ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa Simpson
dialogue.line: 911, what's your emergency?

dialogue.speaker: 911 Operator
dialogue.line: We've had several calls from that address. Let me check the log here... A man stuck in a bathtub with a turkey, someone reporting a flying carpet, and just last week, a complaint about a giant donut on the roof.

jokes.text: Well, near as we can tell, the boy was studying quietly... when the girl, drunk on her own sense of power... beat him silly with a block of frozen lima beans.
jokes.setup: Wiggum explaining what happened to Bart
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong dramatic reconstruction of events
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misinterpretation by Wiggum. The frozen lima beans callback and 'drunk on power' phrase are perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Well, it all started when the boy was riding his skateboard down Evergreen Terrace at approximately 3 PM. He was being chased by what appeared to be a pack of wild dogs, or possibly a single very large dog. The boy then crashed through the front window of the Kwik-E-Mart, destroying several shelves of merchandise. At that point, Apu fired a warning shot, which ricocheted off a fire hydrant and struck the boy in the rear end.

jokes.text: Complete confidentiality? Oh? Well, I'm sure he's as good as Dr. Hibbert. It says so right in his ad.
jokes.setup: Marge reading doctor advertisement
jokes.punchline: Marge's naive trust in advertising claims about medical quality
jokes.explanation: Good Marge naivety about trusting advertisements. The circular logic is appropriately silly.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh my, this doctor has excellent credentials. It says here he's board certified!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: And look, he promises to treat patients with the utmost care and respect.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: I'm sure everything in this advertisement is completely accurate and trustworthy.

jokes.text: Yo, um, I must've, like, fallen on a bullet... and it, like, drove itself into my gut.
jokes.setup: Criminal in emergency room with obvious gunshot wound
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous explanation trying to avoid admitting criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Good Snake characterization with absurd attempt at innocent explanation. The physics of 'driving itself' is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Yeah, man, I was cleaning my gun, and it went off. Then I fell on the bullet. Twice.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: The bullet's still in there.

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I'm saving it for a rainy day, man.

jokes.text: Hey, don't worry. You don't have to make up stories here. Save that for court.
jokes.setup: Doctor responding to Snake's fake story
jokes.punchline: Casual acknowledgment of criminal justice system corruption
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about systemic corruption. Doctor's casual cynicism about perjury is darkly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor Nick
dialogue.line: Well, if a guy's gonna fake his own death, he might as well do it right. The key is bribing the right people.

jokes.text: Excuse me, little lady. The wheelbarrow line's over there.
jokes.setup: Lisa bringing injured Bart in wheelbarrow
jokes.punchline: Hospital apparently has a specific line for wheelbarrow patients
jokes.explanation: Absurd hospital bureaucracy joke. The implication of regular wheelbarrow patients is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Hospital Worker
dialogue.line: We have a special line for wheelbarrow patients.

jokes.text: Oh, loneliness and cheeseburgers are a dangerous mix.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy in emergency room
jokes.punchline: His pretentious way of describing overeating from depression
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy line - turning mundane overeating into philosophical observation. Great character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Comic Book Guy
dialogue.line: The doctor says I need to lose weight. Worst. Diet. Ever.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: Actually, I didn't mention a diet. You just showed up weighing 315 pounds claiming you ate an entire rotisserie chicken while watching the Director's Cut of Blade Runner 2049.

dialogue.speaker: Comic Book Guy
dialogue.line: In my defense, the theatrical cut was severely compromised by studio interference. My depression-driven consumption of poultry was entirely justified by the cinematic context.

jokes.text: Just as I thought! It's a Yard King! That is a quality barrow.
jokes.setup: Wiggum inspecting Lisa's wheelbarrow transportation
jokes.punchline: Wiggum completely distracted by wheelbarrow quality instead of emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - focused on irrelevant details during crisis. The brand specificity is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: A wheelbarrow, eh? Nice ball bearings. Smooth action on the wheels. Where'd you get this beauty?

dialogue.speaker: Lisa Simpson
dialogue.line: Chief Wiggum, we need your help! There's an emergency!

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: In a minute, in a minute. This is one quality piece of equipment. The craftsmanship, the durability... You know, I've been looking for a wheelbarrow just like this.

jokes.text: Citizens of Springfield... I officially declare this- What the hell is that? Why, it's Lisa Simpson! And look what she's doing!
jokes.setup: Mayor making official declaration at ceremony
jokes.punchline: Ceremony interrupted by Lisa's desperate emergency situation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation putting Lisa's private crisis on public display. Mayor's transition from formal to shocked works well.

dialogue.speaker: Mayor Quimby
dialogue.line: I hereby declare this ceremony officially open!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa Simpson
dialogue.line: Wait! Stop! I have an emergency!

dialogue.speaker: Mayor Quimby
dialogue.line: Young lady, I'm in the middle of an official declaration.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa Simpson
dialogue.line: But sir, this is urgent!

dialogue.speaker: Mayor Quimby
dialogue.line: Nothing is more important than municipal procedure.

jokes.text: She's murdered her brother! And she's trying to dump the body in a harbor! Well, duh!
jokes.setup: Crowd misinterpreting Lisa's actions
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of apparent murder as obvious choice
jokes.explanation: 'Well, duh!' response to murder shows Springfield citizens' casual acceptance of violence. Dark but functional.

dialogue.speaker: Crowd Member
dialogue.line: She's killing him!

dialogue.speaker: Another Person
dialogue.line: Well, yeah. What else would she do?

jokes.text: And she's on drugs! Give me the drugs, Lisa.
jokes.setup: Crowd assuming Lisa is on drugs due to her erratic behavior
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate interest in obtaining the drugs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - missing the crisis to focus on personal interest. Shows his priorities clearly.

dialogue.speaker: Crowd Member
dialogue.line: Lisa's on drugs!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Where?!

jokes.text: I was hoping they'd give me one of those steel claws, but what are you gonna do?
jokes.setup: Bart talking about his medical treatment
jokes.punchline: Disappointed he didn't get cool prosthetic instead of healing
jokes.explanation: Perfect kid logic - disappointed injury didn't result in cool robot parts. Bart's casual delivery sells it.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Man, I was really hoping for a cool prosthetic arm or something. But nooo, they just healed it.

jokes.text: Yes, that is a concern, but it's so hard to find a sitter... and I've got judo tonight.
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert acknowledging Lisa's reputation problems
jokes.punchline: Prioritizes personal judo class over child safety concerns
jokes.explanation: Good parental desperation humor. Judo class prioritized over safety shows realistic parent struggles.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Lisa, I'm concerned about your reputation issues at school.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: What should I do, Dr. Hibbert?

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Well, I'd love to help, but I have a personal judo class in ten minutes, and frankly, that's more important.

jokes.text: Yes, that is a concern, but it's so hard to find a sitter... and I've got judo tonight.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks if Dr. Hibbert is concerned about her alleged drug use and violence
jokes.punchline: He acknowledges the concern but prioritizes his judo class
jokes.explanation: Good parenting priorities joke - Dr. Hibbert's personal interests override safety concerns

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Well, yes, I am concerned. But I have a judo class in twenty minutes.

jokes.text: Didn't you hear I almost killed my brother? You did? Just a minute. [Faint Conversation] What time can you come over?
jokes.setup: Lisa warning Ned about her babysitting disaster
jokes.punchline: Ned consults family but still wants to hire her anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending joke about parental desperation overriding safety concerns. The brief consultation makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Mr. Flanders, I have to tell you - last time I babysat, there was a small incident with the house.

dialogue.speaker: Ned
dialogue.line: Well, now, Lisa, I'm sure it wasn't that bad. Let me just consult with the family here.

dialogue.speaker: Maude
dialogue.line: Ned, absolutely not!

dialogue.speaker: Rod
dialogue.line: Yeah, Dad, she's bad!

dialogue.speaker: Todd
dialogue.line: Super bad!

dialogue.speaker: Ned
dialogue.line: Well, the family's spoken. So... when can you start?

tmdb_overview: Lisa has a stellar reputation as a babysitter, but when Homer and Marge leave her in charge of Bart and Maggie, everything starts going wrong.
insight: Babysitting chaos delivers 63 jokes in 22 minutes, anchored by character escalation.
